point_reviews.reason: The document states COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact.
The document confirms that the virus can spread through tiny droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks.
The document mentions that infected individuals can spread the virus even if they have no symptoms.
The document states that droplets from an infected person's breath can land on nearby individuals.
The document describes close contact as including sitting next to someone or sharing a space.
The document indicates that the risk of transmission increases in crowded spaces with poor airflow.
The document states that spending more than 30 minutes in close contact with an infected person raises risk.
The document states COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact.
The document confirms that the virus can spread from an infected person's mouth or nose in small droplets when they cough, sneeze, or breathe.
The document mentions that particles can range in size from larger respiratory droplets to smaller aerosols.
The document states that people can be contagious whether or not they are displaying symptoms.
The document confirms that infection can occur by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after touching contaminated surfaces.
The document states that COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols.
The document confirms that infected individuals exhale virus-containing particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing.
The document mentions that transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances, especially indoors.
The document states that COVID-19 can spread when infectious particles contact the eyes, nose, or mouth.
The document confirms that small airborne particles can remain suspended in the air and travel longer distances indoors.
The document states that people can spread the virus even if they do not show symptoms and remain contagious for up to 20 days.

The external evidence confirms that COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact.
The external evidence supports that the virus can spread through tiny droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks.
The external evidence confirms that infected individuals can spread the virus even if they have no symptoms.
The external evidence supports that droplets from an infected person's breath can land on nearby individuals.
The external evidence confirms that close contact includes sitting next to someone or sharing a space.
